findings.reachability: Any authenticated WordPress user (wp_ajax_ prefix, no nopriv). Exploitable by a logged-in user visiting an attacker-controlled page.
findings.justification: The AJAX handler at line 508 calls add_user_meta() to permanently dismiss an admin notice but performs no nonce verification (check_ajax_referer or wp_verify_nonce), and the corresponding JS at js/black-studio-tinymce-widget.js line 351-356 sends no nonce token, making the action trivially CSRF-triggerable.

detection_pattern.description: Finds wp_ajax_ handler registrations; if the corresponding callback file lacks check_ajax_referer or wp_verify_nonce, the handler has no CSRF protection and any authenticated user can be tricked into triggering it.
